latest.title: Semnet Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3336G
GSTechnologies Ltd
01 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
1 June 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Semnet Update
GSTechnologies Limited (LSE: GST), the fintech company, provides an update to the announcement of 13 February 2026 regarding the Company's 66.67% owned subsidiary Semnet Pte. Ltd ("Semnet").
On 26 May 2026, hearings were held before the Assistant Registrar in the Singapore Supreme Court.
At these hearings, applications for a stay of proceedings numbered SUM 1210 (filed by the Semnet sellers) and SUM 1211 (filed by Chong Hoi Yan- a former Semnet employee), were dismissed in their entirety. A costs order of S$18,000 was awarded to Semnet.
A deadline to file a notice of appeal against the above decisions is set for 9 June 2026. Furthermore, a hearing will be held on 4 June 2026 for the Court to give the defence a direction for their case.
The GST Board remains committed to protecting the interests of GST shareholders and will announce further updates as appropriate.

latest.title: Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Trading Services
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1673Y
GSTechnologies Ltd
26 March 2026
26 March 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Asset Trading Services
GSTechnologies Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces the temporary suspension of the Company's crypto asset trading services with effect from 15 April 2026.
On 31 December 2025 the Company announced that GS Fintech UAB's crypto asset trading platform had transitioned to operate under the VASP-registered entity Finferno Spółka Z Ograniczoną Odpowiedzialnością ("Finferno") in Poland.
Whilst this can continue until 30 June 2026 the Company has determined that the Company's crypto asset trading activities should be aligned with the requirements of the EU Regulation on Markets in Crypto-Assets ("MiCA") as soon as possible.
Accordingly, with effect from 15 April 2026, Finferno will temporarily suspend all services relating to the trading of crypto assets. The suspension will remain in place until a MiCA licence is obtained. Finferno's Terms of Service will be updated in connection with the suspension and will take effect on the same date.
Users of the platform are being informed and further announcements will be made in due course, as appropriate.

latest.title: Semnet Update
latest.content:
13 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8371S
GSTechnologies Ltd
13 February 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
13 February 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Semnet Update
GSTechnologies Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces an update regarding the Company's 66.67% owned subsidiary Semnet Pte. Ltd ("Semnet").
As announced on 18 July 2025, the Company issued a notice of arbitration to Choo Seet EE and Zheng Kang Wen Mervyn (the "Sellers"), the sellers of Semnet, as set out in the Sale and Purchase Agreement ("SPA") dated 5 December 2023. As referenced in the Company's interim results announced on 18 December 2025, a mediation hearing was held in early December 2025 with a view to agreeing a formal settlement agreement between the Sellers and the Company. However, formal settlement terms have not been reached, and consequently, Semnet has now formally issued and served a writ of summons against the Sellers and Hoi Yan ('Daphane') Chong (a former Semnet manager) to vigorously pursue claims for alleged breaches of fiduciary and contractual duties, including breaches of the SPA, that have negatively impacted the Semnet business. The total damages sought are approximately US$4.2 million.
The GST Board remains committed to protecting the interests of shareholders and will announce further updates as appropriate.

latest.title: Completion of Metapay Acquisition
latest.content:
23 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0506Q
GSTechnologies Ltd
23 January 2026
23 January 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Completion of Metapay Acquisition
GSTechnologies
Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, is pleased to announce the successful completion of its acquisition of Metapay SP. z o.o. ("Metapay").
Further to the Company's interim results announcement on 18 December 2025, GST confirms that it has now completed the acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital of Metapay, a company incorporated in Poland.
Metapay holds a Small Payment Institution (SPI) licence and is registered under the Polish Act on Payment Services with MIP260/2025 status. The acquisition is aligned with GST's strategy to enhance its footprint in domestic and cross-border payment services across Europe.
Following completion, the Company has renamed Metapay to Angra Limited sp. z o.o. and, together with the anticipated progress of its licensing initiatives, this is expected to support the continued expansion of Angra Global's service offerings and geographical reach.

latest.title: End of MiCA Transitional Period in Lithuania
latest.content:
31 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 1900N
GSTechnologies Ltd
31 December 2025
31 December
2025
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
End of MiCA Transitional Period in Lithuania
GSTechnologies
Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces that
following the submission of a
MiCA license application to the Bank of Lithuania in September 2025,
GS Fintech UAB has not obtained crypto-asset service provider (CASP) authorisation under MiCA by the end of the transitional period, which expires on 31 December 2025.
The Company plans to resubmit its application to the Bank of Lithuania in Q1 2026 and in the meantime, to ensure trading continuity without interruption, GS Fintech UAB's platform will transition to operations under VASP-registered Finferno Spółka Z Ograniczoną Odpowiedzialnością in Poland, whose acquisition by the Company was announced on 29 December 2025.

company_30s.what_they_do: GST Technologies is a Singapore‑based fintech that offers crypto‑asset trading, wallet services and payment solutions. They run a platform that lets users buy, sell and store digital currencies, and they also provide merchant‑payment APIs for businesses.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The crypto‑payment space is still in its infancy and firms that can combine secure custody with merchant‑integration have a growing niche. GST’s platform sits at the intersection of retail crypto adoption and B2B payment processing, a market that is expected to double in the next five years.
company_30s.current_state: GST has been hit by a series of regulatory hiccups and a high‑profile legal dispute with Semnet. The company has paused crypto trading services for a few weeks, announced a strategic acquisition of Metapay, and is tightening its treasury by holding more Bitcoin. The stock is trading far below its historical highs, reflecting the market’s loss of confidence.

rns_narrative: The company is juggling three major storylines. First, the legal showdown with Semnet has reached a court dismissal, which removes a big uncertainty but also keeps the spotlight on the dispute. Second, GST is pivoting its business model—suspending crypto trading to focus on merchant‑payment APIs and integrating Metapay’s payment network. Third, governance has been in flux, with delayed accounts, a new auditor and a change in voting rights that suggests the board is tightening control.

These themes show a firm trying to survive a regulatory crackdown while repositioning itself as a payment‑tech player. The legal win is a relief, the suspension is a risk‑mitigation step, and the governance changes indicate a drive to rebuild investor confidence.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism: traders are waiting for a court win and a crypto‑service restart.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices praise the court dismissal as a clean break and highlight the Metapay deal as a new revenue stream. They argue that GST can now focus on merchant payments without the volatility of crypto trading.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter points to insider selling, a history of regulatory fines, and the fact that the company has suspended a core part of its business. They warn that the crypto pause could hurt long‑term growth and that the legal case still carries reputational damage.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from panic to cautious hope. Early posts were dominated by fear of regulatory collapse, but recent updates show a potential turnaround, sparking a small but growing bullish thread.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for GST is flat – there’s no public chatter, meaning a good surprise factor if a positive headline breaks.

near_term_catalysts: Court’s final ruling on the Semnet dispute – a clean dismissal could lift sentiment and remove a key blocker.
Resumption of crypto trading services – a return of the core revenue driver would signal operational confidence.
Release of the latest quarterly results – if earnings beat expectations, it would validate the new business focus.
Official announcement of the Metapay integration roadmap – concrete dates for merchant‑payment roll‑out would give the market a clear direction.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden volume spike (twice normal) coupled with a price bounce to the 20‑day moving average. A positive earnings surprise or a regulatory green light will confirm the bounce thesis. Keep an eye on the board’s AGM minutes for any mention of debt restructuring or a potential delisting notice.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early stages of a deep crash. The APEX score of 8.0 suggests a tight compression, so once a catalyst hits, the price could snap back sharply. The key is to confirm that the capitulation phase is over – indicated by a return of institutional volume and a stabilizing support level.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: GST sits at a 70‑95% drop from its peak, a classic set‑up for a mean‑reversion play. The court dismissal removes a major blocker, and the temporary pause on crypto trading is a risk‑management move rather than a sign of collapse. If the company can restore crypto services and start monetising merchant payments, the upside could be 200‑300% from the current base level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Regulatory risk remains – if the court sides with Semnet again or if new crypto‑regulations tighten further, the pause could become permanent. Insider selling suggests a lack of confidence from those closest to the business. A worst‑case scenario would see the stock stay below its 52‑week low for another 12 months.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk is limited to the current price level; upside is potentially triple the entry if the company recovers to mid‑range support. The asymmetry is clear: a small loss if the bounce fails versus a large gain if it succeeds.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a volume spike that exceeds 1.5× the average and the price touches the 20‑day moving average. Allocate 10–15% of your portfolio to this position and set a stop 20% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either (a) a court announcement confirming dismissal, or (b) a press release announcing the resumption of crypto trading. Both should be accompanied by a 2× volume bump.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, announces a delisting, or the court rules against them, exit immediately and consider the position a loss.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GST’s deep crash, a forthcoming court win and the potential restart of crypto services create a high‑reward, low‑downside play for contrarian traders.
